reasoning: The March 2025 snap election in Greenland was triggered by the ruling Siumut party's proposal to accelerate the independence process. The election resulted in a major victory for the gradualist Demokraatit party, which secured roughly 30% of the vote. Demokraatit's platform explicitly emphasizes a slow, measured approach to independence that prioritizes economic stability. Furthermore, coalition talks after the election showed deep divides; the rapid-independence Naleraq party even withdrew from negotiations specifically citing Demokraatit's unwillingness to begin formal independence negotiations. Given this political reality and expert forecasts projecting an independence referendum is unlikely before 2029 (or 2-10 years away), it is highly improbable that the new government will reach an internal consensus and officially announce a referendum date in the remaining ~60 days before the July 1, 2025, deadline. I am using the floor probability of 5% as required by the instructions.
